name: Thanksgiving
description: Pilgrims, parades, presidential pardons and the great American feast.

questions.q: On what day of the week is U.S. Thanksgiving celebrated?

source.notes: Fourth Thursday of November per FDR/Congress 1941.

questions.q: On which Thursday of November is Thanksgiving Day observed?

source.notes: Set by Joint Resolution of Congress in 1941.

questions.q: What was the name of the ship that brought the Pilgrims to America in 1620?

source.notes: Sailed from Plymouth, England in September 1620 with 102 passengers.

questions.q: Approximately how many Pilgrims sailed on the Mayflower?
Which Native American tribe shared the first Thanksgiving meal with the Pilgrims in 1621?

source.notes: Led by Massasoit; ~90 Wampanoag joined ~50 Pilgrims.

questions.q: Approximately how many days did the first Thanksgiving harvest celebration last in 1621?
Which two meats were most likely the centerpiece of the first Thanksgiving — NOT turkey?

source.notes: Wampanoag brought 5 deer; Pilgrims hunted 'fowl' (likely duck/goose).

questions.q: Which U.S. president made Thanksgiving a national holiday in 1863?

source.notes: October 3, 1863 proclamation set the last Thursday of November.

questions.q: Which president moved Thanksgiving from the 'last Thursday' to the 'fourth Thursday' of November in 1941?

source.notes: FDR moved it earlier to extend the Christmas shopping season; codified by Congress 1941.

questions.q: Sarah Josepha Hale, who lobbied for Thanksgiving to become a national holiday, also wrote which famous nursery rhyme?
Which department-store chain has sponsored the famous Thanksgiving Day Parade in NYC since 1924?
What feature was MISSING from the first Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade in 1924?

source.notes: Original 1924 parade used live zoo animals; balloons added 1927.

questions.q: Which U.S. city hosts the OLDEST Thanksgiving Day parade (since 1920)?

source.notes: Originally Gimbels parade; now the 6abc Dunkin' Thanksgiving Day Parade.

questions.q: Which 1955 dish, invented by Campbell's Soup, is a now-classic Thanksgiving casserole?

source.notes: Created by Dorcas Reilly at Campbell's test kitchen.

questions.q: Which sport has been a Thanksgiving Day tradition since the first NFL game in 1920?

source.notes: Detroit Lions and Dallas Cowboys host annually.

questions.q: Which two NFL teams traditionally host Thanksgiving games every year?
choices: Green Bay Packers and Chicago Bears
New York Giants and Pittsburgh Steelers

questions.q: Approximately how many turkeys are eaten in the U.S. on Thanksgiving Day?
Which U.S. president is credited with starting the modern presidential turkey-pardoning tradition in 1989?

source.notes: First official pardon by GHW Bush in 1989. Lincoln spared a turkey for his son Tad in 1863 informally.

questions.q: What is the day after Thanksgiving popularly known as in the U.S.?

source.notes: First major shopping day of the holiday season.

questions.q: On which date is Canadian Thanksgiving celebrated?
